steps.title: Identify the structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: The clause before the blank ends with "ruthenium" and is a complete independent clause. The clause after the blank begins with "the alloy" and is also a complete independent clause. Two independent clauses joined without proper punctuation and a conjunction create a run-on sentence or comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that leave a run-on or splice, and confirm the answer
steps.content:
The standard rule: join two independent clauses with a comma followed by a coordinating conjunction.
  • Choice A — '(Ru)': no punctuation or conjunction — fuses the two clauses into a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — '(Ru) but': conjunction without a comma before it — violates the comma-plus-conjunction convention for longer clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — '(Ru),': comma alone without a conjunction — creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — '(Ru), but': comma and coordinating conjunction "but" — correctly joins the two clauses and signals the contrast between the intended improvement and the unsuitable result. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
